title: Sam Neill Said All Of These Lines. Can You Name The Movie?
dek: In honor of the legendary Sam Neill, who passed away this week at 78, we pulled one iconic line from eight of his most unforgettable roles — no character names, no cheating.

items.reveal_text: Sam Neill's Dr. Alan Grant survives the whole movie, but it's game warden Robert Muldoon who doesn't — <cite index="7-10,7-11">he says this just before he is ambushed and killed by a Velociraptor, realizing one raptor lured him into a trap while another attacked from the side.</cite> It remains one of the most quoted lines in blockbuster history.

items.quote: Some of the worst things imaginable have been done with the best intentions.
items.reveal_text: This time it's Neill's own Alan Grant landing the sharpest line in the movie, delivered as a rebuke <cite index="9-11,9-12">after his colleague Billy steals some Velociraptor eggs and tries to justify it as well-intentioned.</cite> Fans still consider it the franchise's most quietly damning thesis statement.

items.quote: The voice you hear is not my speaking voice, but my mind's voice.
items.reveal_text: Neill played the taciturn husband Stewart opposite Holly Hunter's mute Ada in Jane Campion's Palme d'Or winner. In his own memoir, Neill called it a movie that <cite index="23-1,23-2">he couldn't overestimate the importance of in hindsight, saying it sits on his CV like a medal on his chest.</cite>

items.quote: They tried to kill me, Rae. They tried to suck the life out of me.
items.reveal_text: That paranoid outburst belongs to Billy Zane's unhinged castaway, but it's Neill's frantic husband John Ingram racing to save his wife that drives the film's nail-biting back half. <cite index="31-1,31-3">The thriller starred Neill alongside Nicole Kidman and Billy Zane as a married couple isolated at sea who take in a stranger who's abandoned a sinking ship.</cite>

items.quote: Where we're going, we won't need eyes to see.
items.reveal_text: Neill's Dr. Weir delivers this line moments after his eyes are gouged out by the ship's malevolent influence, in what's often cited as <cite index="15-5,15-7">one of the absolute scariest quotes in sci-fi movie history from a film that was once a box office flop but has since become a cult classic.</cite>

items.quote: Andrew is not a person, he's a form of property.
items.reveal_text: Neill played family patriarch "Sir," the man who ends up championing Robin Williams's android as he pushes for personhood over two centuries. <cite index="49-8">His character recognizes and encourages Andrew's uncommon characteristics, particularly his artistic streak, sensitivity to beauty, humor, and independence of spirit.</cite>

items.quote: I am an artist and I refuse to be compromised by the scruples of the public!
items.reveal_text: Neill played real-life provocateur Norman Lindsay, <cite index="59-3,59-4">a notorious painter living on an Australian estate whose art coexists with an experiment in permissive, free-wheeling living.</cite> Roger Ebert praised the film as a good-hearted look at art and repression, with Neill anchoring the whole bohemian household.

items.quote: And so, Loki died of his wounds, giving his life for ours.
items.reveal_text: Blink and you'll miss it: Neill popped up as the "Asgardian actor" playing Odin in a ridiculous stage play, alongside Matt Damon as Loki and Luke Hemsworth as Thor. <cite index="65-14,65-16">Neill later admitted he was completely baffled shooting the scene, joking that he was truly lost about what was even going on in the Marvel universe.</cite> Rest easy, Sir Sam — and if you want more brain-teasers like this one, keep the guessing going over on Flickle.
